title: veronica spicata subsp. vulgaris
intro: Veronica spicata subsp. vulgaris, also known as common speedwell or Veronica, is a perennial herbaceous plant belonging to the plantain family (Plantaginaceae). The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antiseptic effects. Its health benefits include relieving respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as treating skin conditions like eczema and acne due to its astringent and antiseptic properties. Veronica spicata subsp. vulgaris can be prepared as a tea or tincture for its medicinal uses, and its leaves and flowers can also be used in infusions and salves.
